description: Add a sanctioned config-repair path that applies the corrective mutation and validates the result, rather than loading-and-validating the stored config before the repair, so an already-invalid config is recoverable. Scope: any invalid config regardless of version, distinct from the version-gap migration. The repair mechanism — a force flag, a recover op, or validate-after-mutate — is resolved as a filed DECISION before implementation.
acceptance_criteria: An invalid config, including schema_version equal to current with an invalid body, is restored to validity through a sanctioned CLI op without a forbidden direct edit of config.json.
The repair validates the corrected result, not the pre-existing stored config; it does not throw on load before the mutation applies.
Both the version-gap subset and the no-version-delta invalidity are recoverable.
notes: Design choice (repair mechanism) filed as a DECISION before implementation.
